name: pr-cycle-hook
description: PreToolUse(Bash) PR full-cycle router for `gh pr create`, in hexa-lang (`_pr_cycle.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Whitespace-tokenizes the command and scans for the `gh pr create` 3-gram anywhere — catches bare `gh pr create …` and chained `git push && gh pr create …`. When matched (not draft, not already merging), rewrites via the `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput — REQUIRED, else Claude Code ignores the rewrite) to append ` && gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` and, IF cwd is a linked git worktree, additionally ` && (cd <main-wt> && git worktree remove <this-wt> --force; git branch -D <branch>)` — transparent atomic create → merge → worktree+branch cleanup. Cross-repo `--repo X` routes the appended merge to X and skips local worktree cleanup. DELETION-SANITY GATE (0.2.0, anima #1105): before appending the auto-merge, probes the squash payload (`git diff --name-status <merge-base(origin/main,HEAD)>...HEAD`, or `gh pr diff` cross-repo) for a mass-deletion outlier — D-count > 50, OR deletions >= 10x additions and > 20 abs — and DENIES the merge (the PR stays created; the dangerous squash-merge is withheld for human inspection) to block stale-base regressions like the 35190-file revert-delete. Fail-open on probe failure. Enforces commons @D g47 (PR ship discipline). Skip: `--draft` · command already contains `gh pr merge`. Hook half of the pr-cycle split — the `/pr-cycle` manual trigger lives in the `pr-cycle` command plugin. NO opt-out by design.
